notes.introduction: Preached from James 1:22, this sermon warns against self-deception that comes from hearing the Word of God without doing it, drawing on the concluding illustration of Matthew 7 (the wise and foolish builders) to show the difference between hearers and doers.
outline: I. The Message Delivered — the Sermon on the Mount was delivered perfectly by Jesus Christ Himself (Matthew 7:24, 26)
II. The Message Received — the message was heard by both groups; the problem was not with delivery or reception
III. The Message Applied (or Not) — the single difference between the wise man and the foolish man is 'doeth them' versus 'doeth them not,' determining whether the house stood or fell
notes.conclusion: The preacher closed by urging the congregation not to deceive themselves, stating that the worst deception is self-deception and that we often do not even know when we are doing it. He called on God in prayer to help each person be a doer of the Word, not a hearer only, and expressed the desire to respond to the Spirit with 'yes, Lord, yes.'
notes.application: Come to church asking how God will speak to you personally, not hoping the message will fix someone else. In daily devotions, open the Word and ask specifically: What is God teaching me? What do I need to stop doing? What do I need to start doing? Respond to the invitation at the end of services rather than shying away from it. Commit to being a doer of the Word so that you are not deceiving your own self.
